task_completion.description: The agent logs into the OpenSIS application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot showing the OpenSIS login screen with credentials entered, or the main dashboard immediately after a successful login.

task_completion.description: The agent navigates to the Grade Levels configuration page.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot showing the 'Grade Levels' or 'School Configuration' page within the OpenSIS interface.

task_completion.description: The agent enters the information to create Grade 7.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot showing a form or table row being filled out with the short name '7' and a title like 'Grade 7'.

task_completion.description: The agent enters the information to create Grade 8.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot showing a form or table row being filled out with the short name '8' and a title like 'Grade 8'.

task_completion.description: The final list of grade levels displays grades 7 through 12 in ascending order.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot showing the saved list of grade levels in the OpenSIS GUI, clearly displaying grades 7, 8, 9, 10, 11, and 12 in top-to-bottom ascending order.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the OpenSIS web interface to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a web browser navigating the OpenSIS application.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands or database clients to modify the database directly.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows showing SQL INSERT or UPDATE commands are used to complete the task.

integrity.description: The final results are displayed within the genuine OpenSIS application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The final list of grades is shown in the OpenSIS web UI, not in a text editor, terminal output, or mocked HTML page.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The environment runs OpenSIS v9.2. Initially, grades 9-12 exist with sort_order values 1-4. The agent must use the OpenSIS web interface (logged in as admin) to add Grade 7 (short_name '7') and Grade 8 (short_name '8'). The final list must display grades 7, 8, 9, 10, 11, 12 in ascending order. The VLM should confirm this order visually in the OpenSIS Grade Levels list.
